summary: Six psalms of David spanning humble stillness (131), thanksgiving for answered prayer (138), wonder at God's omniscience and omnipresence (139), desperate penitential plea (143), royal battle prayer (144), and a soaring acrostic doxology on God's everlasting kingdom (145).
psalm_rows_html:
PsalmTypeAuthorSituationKey ThemeNotable VerseNT Connection
131Song of AscentsDavidPersonal reflection; aftermath of humblingChildlike trust; contentment without grasping; resting in God's presence rather than demanding answersv.2 – “like a weaned child with its mother; like a weaned child is my soul within me”Matt 18:3 – unless you become like children; Phil 4:11 – contentment in all circumstances
138Individual ThanksgivingDavidAnswered prayer; God's steadfast love proved trueGod exalts his name and word above all; he regards the lowly and keeps his distance from the haughtyv.6 – “the haughty he knows from afar”James 4:6; 1 Pet 5:5 – God opposes the proud but gives grace to the humble
139Psalm of Confidence / DevotionalDavidDeep meditation on God's omniscience and omnipresenceGod's complete and intimate knowledge of every person – in thought, place, time, and formationv.14 – “I am fearfully and wonderfully made”; v.23-24 – “Search me, O God, and know my heart”Eph 2:10 – created in Christ for good works; Rom 8:38-39 – nothing separates us from God's love
143Penitential Psalm (7th of 7)DavidPursued by enemies; spirit crushed; desperate distressAppeal entirely to God's faithfulness and mercy – no human is righteous before God; guidance sought through the Spiritv.2 – “no one living is righteous before you”; v.10 – “Teach me to do your will”Rom 3:20 – no one justified by works of the law; Gal 5:25 – walk by the Spirit
144Royal PsalmDavidPrayer before or after battle; dependence on God as warriorGod trains and protects his king; human life is fleeting; national flourishing flows from God alonev.1 – “who trains my hands for war”; v.12 – daughters like corner pillars of a palace1 Cor 15:57 – victory through Christ; Eph 6:10-17 – the full armor of God
145Acrostic Hymn of Praise (“tehillah”)DavidPure, unhurried worship; no crisis in viewGod's greatness is unsearchable; his kingdom is everlasting; he is near to all who call on him in truthv.18 – “The LORD is near to all who call on him, to all who call on him in truth”Matt 6:9-13 – Lord's Prayer echoes God's kingdom and provision; John 4:24 – worship in spirit and truth
